description: Auto-generated index of approved architecture guardrails. Do not edit manually — regenerated on every merge to main.

guardrails.title: API Guidelines — Part A: API Concepts and Management 2022
guardrails.description: Comprehensive government API guidelines covering business context, principles, lifecycle management, and governance considerations for API development within public sector organizations. Addresses interoperability, privacy compliance, organizational drivers, and standardization across government agencies.
guardrails.owner: API governance or digital transformation team

source.page_title: API guidelines — Part A: API concepts and management 2022 | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: API Security Guidelines — Part B (NZ Digital Government 2022)
guardrails.description: Comprehensive reference architecture and technical implementation guidance for securing RESTful and modern APIs (GraphQL, AsyncAPI, gRPC). Covers authentication, authorization, identity management, threat protection, and Zero Trust principles for government API deployments.

source.page_title: API guidelines — Part B: API security 2022 | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: API Guidelines Part C: API Development 2022
guardrails.description: Comprehensive technical guidelines for API development covering design principles, HTTP standards, API types (REST, GraphQL, AsyncAPI, gRPC), layering architecture, and implementation standards. Establishes minimum deliverables including interface specifications, code, policies, tests, and SLAs for API projects.

source.page_title: API guidelines — Part C: API development 2022 | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: New Zealand API Standard
guardrails.description: Normative specification for New Zealand government agencies developing, deploying, and operating APIs. Consolidates three NZ API Guideline documents (Parts A, B, C) into a single standard covering design, development, security, deployment, and operations of REST APIs.

source.page_title: The New Zealand API Standard | Version: exposuredraft

guardrails.title: Digital Service Design Standard
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard providing 12 core principles for designing and delivering user-focused, high-quality digital services. Covers user needs, security, privacy, inclusivity, interdisciplinary collaboration, and accountability to ensure ethical and equitable public service delivery.

source.page_title: Digital Service Design Standard | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: NZ Government Cloud First Policy
guardrails.description: New Zealand government's mandatory cloud-first policy requiring public sector organisations to adopt public cloud services for information systems unless specific exceptions apply. Covers data classification requirements, multi-cloud strategies, cost optimization, risk assessment, and sustainability considerations.

source.page_title: Cloud First policy | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: All of Government Cloud Sourcing Strategy
guardrails.description: New Zealand government strategy for procuring secure and resilient cloud services across agencies. Establishes a consolidated Marketplace channel for IaaS and cloud services, introduces security tiering, supports multi-cloud and hybrid approaches, and incorporates Māori data sovereignty and Te Tiriti considerations into cloud procurement.
guardrails.owner: Chief Digital Officer / Digital Government Team

source.page_title: All of Government Cloud Sourcing Strategy | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: CI/CD Flows
guardrails.description: Architecture diagram defining CI/CD pipeline flows and deployment patterns. Documents the standard process for code integration, testing, and release automation within the DevSecOps framework.

guardrails.title: Digital Strategy 2.0 – National Digital Development Framework
guardrails.description: National-level digital strategy document from New Zealand's Ministry of Economic Development outlining the vision and framework for digital development across economy, environment, and society. Covers four pillars: connection, capability, confidence, and content, with emphasis on broadband infrastructure, digital skills, cybersecurity, and inclusive participation.
guardrails.owner: Chief Digital Officer or Digital Strategy Office

guardrails.title: Algorithm Charter for Aotearoa New Zealand
guardrails.description: Government policy framework establishing principles and commitments for responsible algorithm use in New Zealand public services. Defines risk assessment approach, transparency requirements, and safeguards against bias and unintended consequences, particularly for vulnerable communities.
guardrails.owner: Chief Privacy Officer / Data Governance Office

guardrails.title: Principles and Protocols for Producers of Tier 1 Statistics
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard defining 10 core principles and 6 operational protocols for producing official Tier 1 statistics. Covers relevance, integrity, quality, coherence, accessibility, efficiency, privacy protection, respondent management, data frameworks, and statistical records management.

guardrails.title: UN Fundamental Principles of Official Statistics
guardrails.description: UN Economic Commission for Europe resolution establishing 10 core principles for official statistical agencies, covering professional independence, data confidentiality, scientific standards, and international cooperation. Provides foundational governance framework for statistical data collection, processing, and dissemination in democratic societies.

source.filename: United Nations - Fundamental Principles of Official Statistics.pdf

guardrails.title: Privacy Principles Quick Tour — NZ Privacy Act 2020
guardrails.description: Overview of the 13 privacy principles from the New Zealand Privacy Act 2020 governing collection, handling, and use of personal information. Covers lawful purpose, transparency, security, access rights, data accuracy, retention, use limitations, and cross-border transfer requirements.

guardrails.title: Data and Statistics Act 2022 (New Zealand Public Act)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing the framework for collection, management, and use of official statistics and research data across government. Defines roles of Statistics New Zealand and the Government Statistician, sets requirements for data confidentiality and privacy protection, and establishes compliance and enforcement mechanisms.

source.filename: Data and Statistics Act 2022 version 60_0 as at 08 August 2022.pdf

guardrails.title: Data Protection and Use Policy (DPUP) v1.2
guardrails.description: A comprehensive policy framework for the respectful, trusted, and transparent use of personal information in the social sector. Establishes five principles (He Tāngata, Manaakitanga, Mana Whakahaere, Kaitiakitanga, Mahitahitanga) and four guidelines (Purpose Matters, Transparency and Choice, Access to Information, Sharing Value) to guide agencies in data collection, use, and sharing practices.

guardrails.title: Government Procurement Rules 5th Edition 2025
guardrails.description: New Zealand government procurement policy document establishing mandatory rules and principles for responsible expenditure of public funds. Covers the complete procurement lifecycle including planning, market approach, contract award, and system requirements with emphasis on public value, integrity, transparency, and economic benefits to New Zealand.
guardrails.owner: Procurement Team / Government Procurement System Lead

guardrails.title: Health Information Privacy Code 2020
guardrails.description: New Zealand regulatory code governing the collection, storage, use, and disclosure of health information by health agencies, disability support service providers, and related organizations. Establishes 13 core privacy rules aligned with the Privacy Act 2020 and specifies obligations for entities handling personal health data.

guardrails.title: NZGOAL Software Extension: Open Source Licensing Framework for Government Software
guardrails.description: New Zealand Government policy framework for open access and licensing of government-owned software under free and open source software (FOSS) licenses. Provides legal context, policy principles, guidance on selecting appropriate FOSS licenses (permissive vs. copyleft), and a structured review and release process to ensure legally robust public software releases.

guardrails.title: Telecommunications Information Privacy Code 2020
guardrails.description: New Zealand code of practice governing how telecommunications agencies must collect, store, use, and disclose telecommunications information and subscriber data. Establishes 13 privacy rules covering lawful purpose, data security, individual access rights, accuracy, retention, and cross-border disclosure of telecommunications and traffic information.

guardrails.title: New Zealand Data and Information Management Principles
guardrails.description: New Zealand government data and information management principles established in 2011, covering seven core principles: open, protected, readily available, trusted and authoritative, well managed, reasonably priced, and reusable. Defines how government agencies should manage data as a public asset with emphasis on accessibility, quality, stewardship, and lifecycle management.

source.page_title: New Zealand Data and Information Management Principles - data.govt.nz

guardrails.title: Privacy Act 2020 — New Zealand
guardrails.description: New Zealand's primary legislation governing the protection of personal information and individual privacy rights. Establishes information privacy principles, access and correction rights, complaint mechanisms, notifiable breach requirements, and frameworks for information sharing and matching between agencies.

source.page_title: Privacy Act 2020 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: NZ Government Web Accessibility Standard 1.2
guardrails.description: Mandatory standard requiring all NZ public sector departments and Executive branch agencies to meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility requirements for publicly and internally facing web pages, effective 17 March 2025. Includes specific exceptions for complex visual maps, prerecorded media, and inactive pages, with assessment and reporting obligations to the Government Chief Digital Officer.

source.page_title: Web Accessibility Standard 1.2 | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: NZ Government Web Usability Standard 1.4
guardrails.description: Mandatory standard for New Zealand public sector organisations requiring compliance with web usability, identity, contact, copyright, and privacy requirements for publicly and internally facing websites. Effective 17 March 2025, replacing version 1.3.

source.page_title: Web Usability Standard 1.4 | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: NZ Information Sharing Standard — Purpose and Scope
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard (effective 1 July 2025) mandating how public service agencies must establish information sharing agreements with non-government third parties when sharing personal information. Covers purpose, scope, implementation guidance, risk assessment, legal requirements, and template clauses to ensure adequate controls and privacy protection.

source.page_title: Purpose and scope | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: Risk Assessment for Public Cloud Services
guardrails.description: New Zealand government guidance for assessing risks associated with public cloud service adoption, including risk discovery tools, jurisdictional considerations, and organizational risk register updates. Provides frameworks for government agencies to evaluate security controls and approval processes.
guardrails.owner: Chief Information Security Officer or Enterprise Architecture

source.page_title: Risks assessment for public cloud services | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: War Pensions Act 1954 - New Zealand Legislation
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation governing the administration and payment of war pensions and allowances to disabled members of the armed forces, their dependants, and related beneficiaries. Repealed in 2014 by the Veterans' Support Act 2014 but represents the historical legislative framework for veteran support and benefit governance.

source.page_title: War Pensions Act 1954 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Data and Statistics Act 2022 (NZ)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing the statutory framework for collecting, managing, and accessing official statistics and data. Defines roles of Statistics New Zealand and the Government Statistician, governs data collection practices, statistical confidentiality, research access, and enforcement mechanisms for public sector data governance.

source.page_title: Data and Statistics Act 2022 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: State Sector Act 1988 — New Zealand Public Service Governance
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing the governance framework for the State Sector, defining roles and responsibilities of the State Services Commissioner, chief executives, and public service personnel. This Act (now repealed as of August 2020 by the Public Service Act 2020) established foundational principles for public service management, employment conditions, and organizational structure.

source.page_title: State Sector Act 1988 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Social Welfare (Reciprocity Agreements, and New Zealand Artificial Limb Service) Act 1990
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation (repealed 2018) that established reciprocity agreements with other countries for social welfare benefits, veterans' pensions, and superannuation portability, and governed the New Zealand Artificial Limb Service. Administered by Ministry of Social Development.

source.page_title: Social Welfare (Reciprocity Agreements, and New Zealand Artificial Limb Service) Act 1990 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Social Security Act 2018 — New Zealand
guardrails.description: Primary legislation governing social security assistance in New Zealand, including benefit eligibility, rates, beneficiary obligations, and administration by the Ministry of Social Development. Establishes legal framework for jobseeker support, sole parent support, supported living payments, and various allowances.

source.page_title: Social Security Act 2018 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Residential Care and Disability Support Services Act 2018 (NZ)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation governing funding, needs assessment, and means assessment for long-term residential care and home-based disability support services. Establishes rules for determining qualifying persons, contribution liability, and funder responsibility for care costs.

source.page_title: Residential Care and Disability Support Services Act 2018 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Public Records Act 2005 (New Zealand)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing requirements for creation, maintenance, disposal, and public access to public records and local authority records. Defines the role of Archives New Zealand and the Chief Archivist in ensuring government accountability, long-term preservation of records, and public access to official documentation.

source.page_title: Public Records Act 2005 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Ombudsmen Act 1975 — New Zealand Legislation
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing the ombudsmen office and framework for investigating complaints against public service agencies, state enterprises, and local organizations. Defines investigative powers, complaint procedures, secrecy obligations, and coordination with other oversight bodies.

source.page_title: Ombudsmen Act 1975 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Official Information Act 1982 – New Zealand
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing requirements for public access to official information, procedures for information requests, withholding criteria, and oversight mechanisms. Directly applicable to government agencies and organizations handling official information.

source.page_title: Official Information Act 1982 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing the legal framework for superannuation entitlements, the New Zealand Superannuation Fund, the Guardians of New Zealand Superannuation, and the Retirement Commissioner. Administered by the Ministry of Social Development and The Treasury, it defines age and residential qualifications, payment structures, fund management, and oversight mechanisms for retirement income.

source.page_title: New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Human Rights Act 1993 - New Zealand Legislation
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation consolidating human rights protections, anti-discrimination provisions, and complaint resolution mechanisms. Establishes the Human Rights Commission and Human Rights Review Tribunal with authority over employment, goods/services, housing, education, and other discrimination matters across both public and private sectors.

source.page_title: Human Rights Act 1993 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Holidays Act 2003 – New Zealand Employment Leave Legislation
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing minimum entitlements and employer obligations for employee holidays, including annual holidays, public holidays, sick leave, bereavement leave, and family violence leave. Administered by the Ministry of Business, Innovation, and Employment.

source.page_title: Holidays Act 2003 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (New Zealand)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation establishing comprehensive health and safety obligations for persons conducting a business or undertaking (PCBUs), officers, and workers. Covers duty of care, worker participation, hazard management, enforcement mechanisms, and liability frameworks applicable to workplace operations.

source.page_title: Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Employment Relations Act 2000 (New Zealand)
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation governing employment relationships, including provisions on good faith dealings, union recognition, collective bargaining, individual employment agreements, and employee protections. Administered by the Ministry of Business, Innovation, and Employment.

source.page_title: Employment Relations Act 2000 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Oranga Tamariki Act 1989 – Children's and Young People's Well-being
guardrails.description: Primary New Zealand legislation establishing the legal framework for the care, protection, and well-being of children and young persons. Defines powers and duties of the chief executive (Oranga Tamariki—Ministry for Children), establishes family group conference procedures, and sets out grounds and processes for care and protection orders.

source.page_title: Oranga Tamariki Act 1989 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Bill of Rights 1688 — English Constitutional Rights and Liberties
guardrails.description: Imperial Act establishing foundational constitutional rights and liberties, including prohibitions on arbitrary taxation, standing armies, and limits on executive power without parliamentary consent. Foundational legal document for Commonwealth governance frameworks.

source.page_title: Bill of Rights 1688 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Student Allowances Regulations 1998 (New Zealand)
guardrails.description: New Zealand secondary legislation (SR 1998/277) administered by the Ministry of Social Development that establishes eligibility criteria, assessment mechanisms, payment schedules, and suspension rules for student allowances including basic grants, accommodation benefits, and income-based assessments.

source.page_title: Student Allowances Regulations 1998 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Copyright Act 1994 — New Zealand Legislation
guardrails.description: New Zealand legislation consolidating and amending copyright law, covering ownership, duration, infringement, permitted acts, moral rights, and enforcement mechanisms. Relevant for organizations handling creative works, digital content, and intellectual property.

source.page_title: Copyright Act 1994 | New Zealand Legislation

guardrails.title: Responsible AI Guidance for Businesses
guardrails.description: Comprehensive voluntary guidance for New Zealand businesses on adopting and deploying AI systems responsibly. Covers governance, risk management, compliance, data ethics, transparency, and stakeholder engagement across the AI lifecycle, aligned with OECD principles and New Zealand values.

guardrails.title: New Zealand Information Security Manual (NZISM) v3.9
guardrails.description: The NZISM is New Zealand Government's comprehensive information security standard defining baseline and enhanced controls for protecting government information and systems across all classification levels. It establishes certification and accreditation processes, roles and responsibilities, and compliance requirements for government departments, agencies, and entities.

guardrails.title: PSR Information Security (INFOSEC) Policy Framework
guardrails.description: New Zealand Government mandatory information security policy framework defining requirements across four pillars: understanding information assets, designing security measures, validating implementation, and maintaining ongoing security. Applies to all mandated government agencies and recommended as best practice for other organisations.

source.page_title: Information security (INFOSEC) | Protective Security Requirements

guardrails.title: Information Assurance Standard — Entity Information Quality Controls
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard establishing mandatory controls for ensuring entity information collected by relying parties is accurate, distinctive, and suitable for eligibility and capability decisions. Covers information risk assessment, data protection, verification integrity, and fraud prevention across four assurance levels.

source.page_title: Information Assurance Standard | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: Binding Assurance Standard
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard that defines controls for binding entities to their entity information to prevent identity theft and account takeover. Establishes binding assurance levels (1-4) with corresponding factor requirements and ongoing integrity maintenance controls.

source.page_title: Binding Assurance Standard | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: Authentication Assurance Standard
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard defining controls to ensure authenticators remain possessed and solely controlled by authorized holders. Establishes requirements for authentication risk assessment, authenticator holder behavior, binding consistency, and authenticator strength across four assurance levels.

source.page_title: Authentication Assurance Standard | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: Federation Assurance Standard
guardrails.description: This standard establishes controls for Credential Providers and Facilitation Providers in federated identity systems. It addresses risk management, assurance levels, privacy preservation, and secure credential presentation to reduce identity theft, entitlement fraud, and misrepresentation.

source.page_title: Federation Assurance Standard | NZ Digital government

guardrails.title: New Zealand Government Cloud Adoption and Risk Assessment Framework
guardrails.description: Outlines the New Zealand government's mandatory framework for public cloud service adoption, including information classification, risk assessment requirements, and compliance with the Privacy Act 2020 and Information Security Manual. Government organizations must conduct due diligence using the Risk Discovery Tool covering data sovereignty, security, privacy, and incident response.

source.page_title: New Zealand Government Information Security and Privacy Considerations - Microsoft Compliance | Microsoft Learn

guardrails.title: Information Assurance Standard — NZ Digital Government
guardrails.description: New Zealand government standard establishing information management controls to ensure collected entity information is accurate, distinctive, and suitable for eligibility and capability decisions. Provides four-level assurance framework (IA1–IA4) for risk-based verification of identity information, covering evidence quality, storage, accuracy, and counter-fraud measures.
guardrails.owner: Identity and Access Management / Security Architecture

source.page_title: Information Assurance Standard | NZ Digital government
